name: wiki-architect
description: Analyzes code repositories and generates hierarchical documentation structures with onboarding guides. Use when the user wants to create a wiki, generate documentation, map a codebase structure, or understand a project's architecture at a high level.
capabilities: Analyze repository structure and identify architectural patterns
Generate hierarchical JSON catalogues with nested documentation sections
Create principal-level guides with architecture diagrams and tradeoffs
Build zero-to-hero learning paths for new developers
Produce getting started guides with setup and usage instructions
Cite specific files and line numbers in all documentation prompts
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 13 potential issues, all confirmed as false positives after review. External command patterns were Markdown code spans using backticks for inline code formatting. Cryptographic and reconnaissance patterns were word matches in documentation text. No actual security risks found.
valueStatement: Transform complex codebases into organized, hierarchical documentation with structured learning paths. Automate wiki creation with architecture diagrams, onboarding guides, and developer documentation.
userTitle: Generate Wiki Documentation from Any Codebase
limitations: Cannot execute code or test runtime behavior during analysis
Limited to static analysis of file structure and content
Requires existing codebase structure to generate meaningful documentation
May need human review for complex domain-specific terminology
Small repositories (10 files or fewer) use simplified structure

useCases.title: New Team Member Onboarding
useCases.description: Create comprehensive onboarding guides that help new developers understand codebase architecture, key components, and contribution workflows from day one.

useCases.title: Legacy System Documentation
useCases.description: Generate structured documentation for existing projects lacking proper docs, mapping architectural layers and component relationships automatically.

useCases.title: Open Source Wiki Creation
useCases.description: Produce professional wikis and documentation sites for open source projects to improve contributor experience and user adoption.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Wiki Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a wiki for this repository
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick start request for basic documentation structure

promptTemplates.title: Onboarding Guide Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate an onboarding guide for this codebase that helps new developers understand the architecture and start contributing
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating learning paths for new team members

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this repository and produce a hierarchical documentation catalogue with architecture diagrams, component breakdowns, and file references
promptTemplates.scenario: Deep technical documentation with architectural insights

promptTemplates.title: Complete Wiki with Learning Paths
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive wiki including principal-level architecture guide, zero-to-hero learning path, getting started documentation, and deep dive into subsystems with specific file references
promptTemplates.scenario: Full documentation suite for complex projects

bestPractices: Provide clear project context when invoking the skill, such as project type and primary goals
Review generated documentation for accuracy and add domain-specific knowledge
Use the output as a template and customize based on team standards and conventions
Keep the generated wiki updated as the codebase evolves
antiPatterns: Expecting runtime behavior analysis without executing the codebase
Using this skill for non-code projects or pure content repositories
Assuming generated documentation replaces human-written explanations entirely
Invoking the skill on empty or minimally populated repositories

faq.question: What types of projects can Wiki Architect analyze?
faq.answer: Wiki Architect works with any codebase including web applications, APIs, libraries, and tools. It detects languages and frameworks from file extensions and build configuration to generate appropriate documentation structures.

faq.question: How does Wiki Architect handle small versus large repositories?
faq.answer: Small repositories with 10 or fewer files receive simplified documentation with Getting Started and Onboarding sections only. Larger repositories get full hierarchical catalogues with Deep Dive sections covering all architectural layers.

faq.question: Can I customize the generated documentation structure?
faq.answer: Yes. The output is a JSON catalogue that serves as a template. You can modify the structure, add sections, adjust prompts, and refine content to match your team's documentation standards and project requirements.

faq.question: Does Wiki Architect execute my code to understand behavior?
faq.answer: No. Wiki Architect performs static analysis only, examining file structure, names, and content. It does not execute code or analyze runtime behavior, keeping the analysis safe and fast.

faq.question: What file formats and programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill detects languages from file extensions and works with any programming language. It automatically selects appropriate comparison languages for cross-learning explanations based on the detected primary language.

faq.question: How accurate are the file references in generated documentation?
faq.answer: File references are based on actual repository structure at the time of analysis. As your codebase changes, regenerate documentation to keep citations current. Always verify references match your current codebase state.
